rulings.Case Title: SCHAPIRO-THORN INC., VS. DANIEL L. MITCHELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default Of Cross-Defendant Suzie Thorn With Respect To The Cross-Complaint Of Defendant Cross-Complainant Daniel L. Mitchell, And For Sanctions In The Amount Of At Least $3,000
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, February 26 2015, Line 1, CROSS DEFENDANT THORN, SUZIE S. Motion To Set Aside Default Of Cross-Defendant Suzie Thorn With Respect To The Cross-Complaint Of Defendant Cross-Complainant Daniel L. Mitchell, And For Sanctions In The Amount Of At Least $3,000. Granted. The default entered against Cross-Defendant Suzie Thorn is set aside. Because Thorn was not served with notice of entry of the order denying the motion to quash, the time to file a responsive pleading did not commence. (See CCP sec. 418.20(b).) Thorn is ordered to file a responsive pleading within 10 days of entry of this order. The request for sanctions pursuant to CCP sec. 128.5 is denied. "Section 128.5¿was superseded for actions filed on or after January 1, 1995 by section 128.7." (Clark v. Optical Coating Laboratory, Inc. (2008) 165 Cal.App.4th 150, 164.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM F RYDER VS. SATEL LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, February 26 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANT SATEL LLC, RICHARD HYLEN Motion For Sanctions. Motion denied. Defendants have failed to make a showing that Plaintiff's filing of his Complaint was frivolous. The evidence here does not establish the amount of the promissory note, and no bad faith motive of Plaintiff has been demonstrated. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is granted, and sanctions are awarded in favor of Plaintiff in the amount of $12,033. Defendants' Motion for Sanctions was frivolous, because the evidence submitted in support does not come close to establishing that Plaintiff's Complaint was filed in bad faith or that it lacks merit. Further, Plaintiff attempted on several occasions to persuade Plaintiff to not file or to withdraw the Motion, and the grounds in support. rulings.Case Title: CANDLESTICK VIEW HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION, VS. J.L. BUILDERS, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, February 26 2015, Line 7, DEFENDANT A&B ROOFING, INC. DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. The court defers to plaintiff's construction of the prime contract, as set forth in Paragraph 17 of the Second Amended Complaint. See Aragon-Haas v. FamilySecurity Ins. Services, Inc., (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 232, 239 ("While plaintiffs' interpretation of the contract may prove invalid, it [is] improper to resolve the issue against her solely on her own pleading. 'In ruling on a demurrer, the likelihood that the pleading will be able to prove his allegations is not the question.'"). Art. 27 of the prime contract indicates that the plaintiff could not compel the subcontractors to arbitrate. Accordingly, defendant has not shown that its negligence was something litigable in the prior arbitration and res judicata applies. In Thibodeau v. Crum (1992) Cal.App.4th 749, 761, the court expressly found that the subcontract was subject to the prime contract and all of the parties were obliged to arbitrate. It is premature for the court to make that finding in this case. rulings.Case Title: ROBERT HALF INTERNATIONAL, INC. A DELAWARE CORPOR VS. DONNA FARRUGIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, February 26 2015, Line 13, DEFENDANT BRIAN GABRIELSON DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. The court finds that California law applies. Ms. Farrugia/Nelson/Klatt made their solicitations while in California and enticed the employees to come to California. California employers have a strong policy in favor of recruiting talented employees to work in California. In addition, plaintiff did not provide any choice of law analysis. The court overrules the demurrer to the breach of contract cause of action. Paragraph 57 of the 2nd Amended Complaint alleges that Gabrielson took plaintiff's documents. Civil Code sec. 3426.7(b) provides that CUTSA does not displace contractual remedies. Business and Professions Code sec. 16600 does not allow an employee to take an employer's property. The court overrules the demurrer as to the UCL cause of action. Plaintiff alleges that all of the defendants (including Ms. Farrugia who allegedly stole trade secrets) acted together in an unlawful and unfair manner. rulings.Case Title: ROBERT HALF INTERNATIONAL, INC. A DELAWARE CORPOR VS. DONNA FARRUGIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, February 26 2015, Line 13, DEFENDANT DONNA FARRUGIA, GARY D NELSON ASSOCIATES INC., D/B/A NELSON FAMILY DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. The court finds that California law applies. Ms. Farrugia/Nelson/Klatt made their solicitations while in California and enticed the employees to come to California. California employers have a strong policy in favor of recruiting talented employees to work in California. In addition, plaintiff did not provide any choice of law analysis. The demurrer to the 1st cause of action for breach of contract is overruled. Plaintiff can base the claim on paragraph 8 of the contract [covenant to protect trade secrets]. Pars. 36-37 and 39 of the 2nd Amended Complaint allege that Ms. Farrugia stole plaintiff's trade secrets (e.g. business plans) and wrongfully used that information. Stealing trade secrets does not implicate Business & Professions Code sec. 16600. The court overrules the demurrer to the intentional interference and UCL causes of action. As stated, the allegations that Ms. Farrugia stole trade secrets to effectuate her scheme supports the claims. rulings.Case Title: ROBERT HALF INTERNATIONAL, INC. A DELAWARE CORPOR VS. DONNA FARRUGIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, February 26 2015, Line 13, DEFENDANT DONNA FARRUGIA, GARY D NELSON ASSOCIATES INC., D/B/A NELSON FAMILY MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. The court grants and denies the motion to strike in part. Ms. Farrugia's notice of motion to strike lists numerous allegations to strike, but does not clearly explain why they are improper. The gist of the motion is to strike the allegations regarding paragraphs 8, 11, and 13 of the contract. The motion is denied as to the paragraph 8 allegations. The covenant to protect the trade secrets is enforceable and does not run afoul of Business and Professions Code sec. 16600. The motion is granted with leave to amend as to the allegations relating to paragraph 11 of the contract [non-solicitation of employee clause]. A contractual provision preventing a departing employee from "raiding" his former employer's employees is "not void on its face under Business and Professions Code section 16600." Loral Corp. v. Moyes, 174 Cal.App.3d 268, 280 (1985). Fillpoint, LLC v. Mass (2012) 208 Cal.App.4th 1170 is distinguishable because it was a sale of business case. Plaintiff's allegations regarding Mr. Gabrielson and Mr. Klatt appear improper. Paragraph 11 prohibits the solicitation of an "Other Employee." The contract defines an "other employee" as working in an "Applicable Office." An "Applicable Office" is one that Ms. Farrugia worked in or had supervisory authority over. Ms. Farrugia did not work with Mr. Gabrielson or Mr. Klatt. The court grants the motion to strike without leave to amend as to the allegations regarding paragraph 13 of the contract [wrongful use]. The covenant seeks to hamper the employees' attempt to engage in their trade and facially violates Business and Professions sec. 16600. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ROBERT HALF INTERNATIONAL, INC. A DELAWARE CORPOR VS. DONNA FARRUGIA et al

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, February 26 2015, Line 13, PLAINTIFF ROBERT HALF INTERNATIONAL, INC. MOTION TO STRIKE DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. The motion is denied. It was unclear when the defendant's were supposed to respond to the Second Amended Complaint. Defendants were never served with a notice of entry of order. In addition, the merits of the parties' dispute have been briefed and defendant could subsequently bring a motion for judgment on the pleadings to raise many of the same issues. rulings.Case Title: ROBERT HALF INTERNATIONAL, INC. A DELAWARE CORPOR VS. DONNA FARRUGIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, February 26 2015, Line 13, DEFENDANT ABRAHAM KLATT DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. The court finds that California law applies. Ms. Farrugia/Nelson/Klatt made their solicitations while in California and enticed the employees to come to California. California employers have a strong policy in favor of recruiting talented employees to work in California. In addition, plaintiff did not provide any choice of law analysis. The court overrules the demurrer to breach of contract cause of action. Plaintiff can allege a breach based on paragraphs 8 and 11 of the contract. Civil Code sec. 3426.7(b) provides that CUTSA does not displace contractual remedies. Plaintiff alleges that Mr. Klatt supervised Ms. Rupp at paragraph 34 of the complaint ad solicited her. A contractual provision preventing a departing employee from "raiding" his former employer's employees is "not void on its face under Business and Professions Code section 16600." Loral Corp. v. Moyes, 174 Cal.App.3d 268, 280 (1985). Fillpoint, LLC v. Mass (2012) 208 Cal.App.4th 1170 is distinguishable because it was a sale of business case. The court overrules the demurrers to the intentional interference and UCL causes of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: ESTATE OF ZHEN GUANG NG ET AL VS. GIAMPAOLO BOSCHETTI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, February 26 2015, Line 15, DEFENDANT GIAMPAOLO BOSCHETTI DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained in part and overruled in part. As to the demurrer on uncertainty grounds, the demurrer is overruled. The First Amended Complaint adequately pleads that the children of the decedent bring the first two causes of action under a wrongful death theory. The successor-in-interest of the estate brings the third cause of action under a survival theory that incorporates the allegations in the first two causes of action. As to the demurrer on the grounds of failure to comply with CCP sec. 377.32, the Court grants Plaintiffs' request for judicial notice and overrules the demurrer as moot. As to the demurrer to the punitive damages cause of action, the demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. "There is no cause of action for punitive damages." (Hillard v. A.H. Robins Co. (1983) 148 Cal.App.3d 374, 391. Rather, punitive damages are a remedy. Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend to allege these facts under one of the other causes of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: LAURIE POLK VS. COMMUNITY HOUSING PERTNERSHIP
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Community Housing Partnership'S Motion For Bifurcation Of Discovery (Per Stipulation Of Parties)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 26, 2015, Line 5, Defendant Community Housing Partnership's Motion For Bifurcation Of Discovery (Per Stipulation Of Parties). Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalonotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The parties entered into a stipulation to continue the hearing. No hearing required. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcc@manningllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =(302/JPT)
